folio.label: 93r
folio.l: Taketú his first spryngynge , and his courssoursc sours
That Estward ay , encresitú in his cours
To EmeleEmelieEmelie ward , To Feraro and venyse
The whicú a long thyngö were , to deuyse
And trewely , as to my Iugement
Me thynketú it a thyngö . , impartinent
Saue he wole conueyen , his matere
But this is the tale , whicú æ ©e moun~ heere
¢ Hic definit ¸logus , et incipit fabula C&lbar;ici de Oxonö ;
THere is rigút at the west side , of Ytaille
Doun at the rote , of vesulus the colde
A lusty pleyn , habundaunt of vytaille
Where many a Toun~ & Tour , æè mayst byholde
That founded were , in tyme of fadres olde
And many a nother , delitable sygút
And Saluces , this noble contree hygút
¢ A Markys whylom , lord was of that londe
As were his worthy elderes , hi~ byfore
And obeissau~t , ay redy to his honde
Were alle hise lieges , bothe lesse and more
Thus in delyt he lyuetú , and hatú doon ©ore
Byloued and dred , thurgú fauour of fortune
Bothe of his lordes , & of his Comune
¢ There witú he was , to speken of lynage
The gentillest I born~ , of lumbardye
A fair ¹sone and strongö , and ©ongö of age
And ful of honour , and curteysye
Discret I now , his contree for to gye
Saue in so~me thynges , he was to blame
And WaxlterWalterwaxlter , was this ©ongö lordes name
¢ I blame hi~ thus , that he considered nougút
In tyme comyngö , what mygút hi~ betyde
But on his lust päsent , was al his thougút
As for to hauke and hunte , on euäy syde
Wel ny , alle othere cures , leet he slide
And eke he nolde , and that was werst of alle
Wedde no wyf , for nougút that mygút byfalle
¢ Only that poynt , his peeple bar so sore
That flokmele on a day , to hiö they went
And oon of hem , that wysest was of lore
Or elles , that the lord best , wolde assent
That he shulde telle hi~ , what the peeple ment
Or elles coude he shewe wel , swicú mateer
He to the Markys seyde , as ©e shuln heere
